slack-guide.seo-description: How to create a Slack Incoming Webhook URL for alerts.
slack-guide.title: Set up Slack alerts
slack-guide.intro: Create a Slack app, enable Incoming Webhooks, and copy the webhook URL into Alerts.

before.workspace: Use a Slack workspace where you can create or install apps.
before.channel: Choose the channel that should receive alerts before creating the webhook.
before.private: Keep the webhook URL private. Anyone with it can post to that channel.
before.recreate: Create a new webhook if you need to move alerts to another channel.

steps.title: Create a Slack app
steps.text: Open the Slack API app page and create a new app for the workspace where alerts should be posted.
images: Open Your Apps in Slack API and click Create an App.
Choose From scratch so you can configure the app manually.
After the app is created, open Incoming Webhooks from the left menu.

steps.title: Enable Incoming Webhooks
steps.text: Incoming Webhooks let Alerts post messages into a Slack channel using a private webhook URL.
images: Turn Activate Incoming Webhooks on for the app.
Scroll to Webhook URLs for Your Workspace and click Add New Webhook.

steps.title: Choose a channel and copy the webhook URL
steps.text: Select the Slack channel that should receive alerts, allow the app, and copy the generated webhook URL.
images: Choose the channel for the webhook, then click Allow.
Copy the new Webhook URL from the Incoming Webhooks page.

steps.title: Paste the webhook into Alerts
steps.text: Return to Alerts, choose Slack, enter a private channel name, paste the webhook URL, and save the channel.
images: Paste the Slack Incoming Webhook URL into the Webhook URL field in Alerts.

x-guide.seo-description: How to create X API keys for alerts.
x-guide.title: Set up X alerts
x-guide.intro: Create an X Dev Console app, enable write permissions, and copy the four required keys into Alerts.

before.account: Use an X account dedicated to publishing alerts.
before.write: The app must have Read and write permissions.
before.balance: Add credits to the X Dev Console account; the bot will not publish without funds.

steps.title: Create or open your X account
steps.text: Use the X account that should publish alerts. The screenshots show the full new-account flow, including email confirmation and profile setup.
images: Open X and choose Create account, or sign in if the bot account already exists.
Enter the account name, email address, and date of birth.
Choose the X customization options and continue.
Review the account details and click Sign up.
Complete the X account authentication check.
Copy the verification code from the email sent by X.
Add the profile picture for the account, or continue with your chosen setup.
Confirm the account opens to the X home page.

steps.title: Apply for X developer access
steps.text: Open X Dev Console at console.x.com/onboarding and complete the developer agreement with a plain description of the alerts bot.
images: Fill in the account name and use case, accept the developer agreement, and submit the form.

steps.title: Open the app and save the Consumer Key pair
steps.text: After developer access is active, open Apps in X Dev Console and select your app. In Keys and Tokens, generate the OAuth 1.0 Consumer Key pair and copy it into Alerts as API Key and API Key Secret.
images: On the X Dev Console dashboard, open Apps. Add credits to the X Dev Console balance before using the bot.
Select the app that will publish Bithomp alerts.
In Keys and Tokens, click Regenerate for the OAuth 1.0 Consumer Key.
Copy Consumer Key and Consumer Key Secret, then paste them into API Key and API Key Secret in Bithomp Alerts.

steps.title: Enable write permissions
steps.text: The app must have Read and write permissions before it can publish alerts. Set permissions before generating the Access Token so the token is created with write access.
images: Open User authentication settings and click Set up.
Select Read and write permissions.
Choose Web App, Automated App or Bot as the app type.
Fill in Callback URI / Redirect URL and Website URL, then save the changes.
X shows OAuth 2.0 Client ID and Client Secret after saving. These are not the four Alerts channel fields.

steps.title: Copy the keys into Bithomp Alerts
steps.text: Return to Keys and Tokens after Read and write is saved. Generate the Access Token pair, paste all four values into the matching Bithomp Alerts fields, and make sure the X Dev Console account has credits.
images: Confirm the Access Token row shows Read and write, then create the Access Token pair.
Copy Access Token and Access Token Secret, then paste them into the matching Bithomp Alerts fields.
Save the Bithomp channel with API Key (Consumer Key), API Key Secret (Consumer Key Secret), Access Token, and Access Token Secret. X Dev Console also needs credits for the bot to work.
